title: Oden Sails with Roger Toward the World’s Truth
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Kiku, Ashura Doji, Denjiro, Unknown person #10, Franky as Oden travels with Roger, reaches the truth of the world, and returns to a Wano already being poisoned by Orochi and Kaido.
scene: Oden travels with Roger, reaches the truth of the world, and returns to a Wano already being poisoned by Orochi and Kaido.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Is that so? Thank you, everyone!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No, please, we did it because we truly love (I often-san,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Right?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's not fair! I want to be popular too!!

dialogue.speaker: Kiku
dialogue.text: Kiku! Regarding IZou...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Oh, inu-chan and neko-chan already told me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I am sure he will be happy!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I see!

dialogue.speaker: Ashura Doji
dialogue.text: Where are denjiro and Ashura?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We have not spoken in a year after all...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ashura has returned to his bandits in the mountains.

dialogue.speaker: Denjiro
dialogue.text: Meanwhile, denjiro due to his con artistry,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Never stays in one place for too long.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: they have returned to their old lifestyles, I see.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: That's nothing to be proud of.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Yes...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We are overjoyed to have you back for another reason as well.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: We'd like to start from the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: After your departure,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Kuri was left without its Daimyo, but it endured thanks to the support of it's people.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It seems Father's condition was worse than I imagined...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I truly regret not being there for him in his final moments...!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: The problem is with the chosen successor...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: For some reason, Sukiyaki-sama appointed Croch/ as the new Shogun...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: their story threw me into great confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Please lend me some money...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Orochi is like a brother to me?! I only ever lent him money out of respect for Yasu-san!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He never even returned what he owed me.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: evenmore surprisingly, he is a member of the Kurozumi family.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Kurozumi Crochi... to think of all people, he was the one appointed to lead in my stead.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I am sure you saw it on your way here...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Weapon factories have been built in every village,

dialogue.speaker: Franky
dialogue.text: Each has their own supervisors.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: under the Shogun's orders, all of the men are forced to work in them,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And their pay is so feeble they could never feed themselves or their families!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But why?! Why did anyone take orders from Orochi to begin with?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: that would be... because Orochi has the support of a pirate called "Ka/Do".

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That pirate! His strength is like that of a monster!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Orochi only commands because he relies on KA/DO...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...that's right!! We attempted to fight back but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Remember somebody

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: No! Don't say anything more about it!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: No, speak up!! What happened?!

dialogue.speaker: Raizo
dialogue.text: RAIZO!! Take momonosuke- SAMA and Hiyori-SAMA outside!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Understood!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: six months ago, a man refused to work at the weapon factory.

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: Further pushed by Orochi's continuous violence...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We could no longer control our anger! We left to stage an attack on Orochi at the flower capital, but...!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We were shocked to find that Kaido's forces were already in Kuri!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Then they raided the castle....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Their goal was to kill the next successor of the Kozuki clan, MOMONOSUKE- SAMA!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Fortunately... Kawamatsu and Inuarashi had stayed behind, and they managed to repel their forces. However...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: competence, though,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Toki-sama used her body as a shield, and was struck in the leg by the initial arrow...!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: We cannot stone for our failures!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Because of our thoughtless actions, we allowed your family's safety to be jeopardized!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and toki-sama has suffered a permanent scar on her leg!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden, Roger, and the Final Voyage storyline with 57 dialogue beats. Oden travels with Roger, reaches the truth of the world, and returns to a Wano already being poisoned by Orochi and Kaido.
